event: Exterior view of Santa Maria delle Grazie church in Milan
symbols: church building — the house of God and gathering place of the faithful
dome — the heavens above the worshipping community
brick and stone construction — the endurance of faith through centuries
description: A photograph of the exterior of Santa Maria delle Grazie, a brick church in Milan with a distinctive Renaissance dome and apse designed by Bramante. The left portion shows the Gothic-style red brick nave with arched windows and a simple triangular facade with a round window. The right portion features the dramatic domed tribune in red brick with white stone detailing. Trees and pedestrians are visible in the foreground on a sunny day.
theological_description: Santa Maria delle Grazie is renowned as the home of Leonardo da Vinci's Last Supper, making it one of the most significant Christian pilgrimage sites in the world. The church, dedicated to the Virgin Mary ('Our Lady of Grace'), embodies the theological conviction that sacred spaces exist to mediate God's grace to the faithful. The juxtaposition of the simpler Gothic nave with Bramante's grand Renaissance tribune illustrates the continuity of worship across changing architectural styles — the same faith expressed in evolving forms. As a house of worship containing one of the most iconic depictions of the Eucharist's institution, this building serves as a permanent reminder of Christ's command: 'Do this in remembrance of me' (Luke 22:19).

scripture_refs.reason: Do this in remembrance of me — the church houses Leonardo's Last Supper, the Eucharistic institution
How lovely is your dwelling place, Lord Almighty — the beauty of this sacred house
